agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "A logistics platform introducing automated route optimization for drivers at shift start, with dynamic recalculation triggers, customer proximity notifications, vehicle-class enforcement, and configurable stop duration. This spans multiple bounded contexts: route planning, fleet/driver management, delivery lifecycle, and customer notifications.",
    "bounded_context": "Primary: Route Planning. Adjacent: Fleet Management, Delivery Management, Customer Notification",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Zone", "definition": "A geographic partition of the service area used to scope deliveries and driver assignments. A driver operates within exactly one zone per shift." },
      { "term": "Optimized Route", "definition": "An ordered sequence of delivery stops calculated to minimize total travel time/distance for all pending deliveries within a driver's assigned zone." },
      { "term": "Pending Delivery", "definition": "A delivery that has been accepted into the system but not yet completed, failed, or cancelled — eligible for route assignment." },
      { "term": "Shift", "definition": "A bounded work period during which a driver is active and eligible for delivery assignments. Route optimization is triggered at shift start." },
      { "term": "Route Recalculation", "definition": "The process of re-optimizing the remaining stop sequence in response to a defined trigger event (new delivery, failed delivery, manual deviation)." },
      { "term": "Next 3 Stops Window", "definition": "A proximity threshold defined as the three upcoming stops in the driver's current route sequence, used to trigger customer notifications." },
      { "term": "Licensed Vehicle Class", "definition": "A classification of vehicle capability (e.g., motorcycle, van, truck, freight) that constrains which deliveries a driver may be assigned. Belongs to Fleet Management context." },
      { "term": "Manual Deviation", "definition": "When a driver physically departs from the system-suggested route sequence, detected via GPS telemetry or the driver skipping/reordering stops." },
      { "term": "Failed Delivery", "definition": "A delivery attempt that did not result in successful handoff to the recipient. Transitions to a terminal or retry state and is removed from the active route." },
      { "term": "Estimated Stop Duration", "definition": "The time budget allocated per delivery stop for route time calculations. Defaults to 4 minutes, overridable by operations team at the zone level." },
      { "term": "Vehicle Class Constraint", "definition": "An invariant rule within Fleet Management that prevents assignment of deliveries requiring a vehicle class exceeding the driver's licensed class." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "When a driver starts their shift, the system must automatically generate an optimized route from all pending deliveries currently in the driver's assigned zone.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The trigger (shift start) and scope (all pending deliveries) are policy decisions. Operations could decide to batch differently or trigger at different points.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given N pending deliveries in zone Z when driver D starts shift, then driver D receives a route containing exactly those N deliveries in an optimized order within T seconds."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "The route must be recalculated when a new delivery is added to the driver's active zone.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a policy choice about when to recalculate. Operations could choose to batch new deliveries or defer recalculation.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given driver D has an active route in zone Z, when a new pending delivery is created in zone Z, then driver D's route is recalculated to include the new delivery."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "The route must be recalculated when a delivery on the active route is marked as failed.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Policy decision. The system could alternatively just skip the failed stop without full recalculation.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given driver D has an active route containing delivery X, when delivery X is marked failed, then driver D's route is recalculated excluding delivery X."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "The route must be recalculated when the driver manually deviates from the suggested route.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Policy decision about how aggressively to re-optimize. Could alternatively just adjust ETA without full recalculation.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given driver D has an active route, when deviation is detected (driver skips a stop or goes off-path beyond threshold), then the route is recalculated from the driver's current position."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "Customers must be notified when their delivery enters the 'next 3 stops' window of the driver's current route.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The notification threshold (3 stops) is a configurable business policy. Could be changed to time-based or distance-based.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given delivery X is at position N in driver D's route, when the driver completes the stop at position N-3, then the customer for delivery X receives a notification."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "A driver must never be assigned a delivery whose shipment requirements exceed their licensed vehicle class.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a physical and legal constraint. A motorcycle physically cannot carry freight. Violating this creates safety hazards, regulatory violations, and guaranteed delivery failures. No policy change can override physics or law.",
      "testable_assertion": "For every delivery D assigned to driver V, assert that delivery D's required vehicle class is equal to or lower than driver V's licensed vehicle class. Any assignment violating this must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "The estimated time per delivery stop defaults to 4 minutes but can be overridden by the operations team at the zone level.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The default value and override mechanism are both configurable policy decisions.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given zone Z has no override, route calculations for zone Z use 4-minute stop duration. Given zone Z has an override of 6 minutes, route calculations for zone Z use 6-minute stop duration."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "A driver can only be assigned deliveries within their assigned zone.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Zone assignment is a fundamental organizational constraint ensuring coverage, accountability, and operational control. Cross-zone assignment would corrupt route optimization and zone-based metrics. If a delivery needs to move zones, that is a deliberate reassignment, not an assignment violation.",
      "testable_assertion": "For every delivery D assigned to driver V in zone Z, assert that delivery D's zone equals Z."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [
    {
      "id": "AC-1",
      "given": "A driver starts their shift and there are 5 pending deliveries in their zone",
      "when": "The shift-start event is processed",
      "then": "The driver receives an optimized route containing all 5 deliveries, ordered to minimize travel time/distance"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-2",
      "given": "A driver has an active route with 8 remaining stops",
      "when": "A new delivery is added to their zone",
      "then": "The route is recalculated to include the new delivery among the remaining stops in an optimized order"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-3",
      "given": "A driver has an active route and their next stop is delivery X",
      "when": "Delivery X is marked as failed",
      "then": "Delivery X is removed from the route and the remaining stops are recalculated from the driver's current position"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-4",
      "given": "A driver's route says to go to stop A next, but the driver drives toward stop C instead",
      "when": "The system detects the deviation",
      "then": "The route is recalculated from the driver's actual current position with all remaining undelivered stops"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-5",
      "given": "A customer's delivery is at position 5 in the driver's route and the driver just completed position 2",
      "when": "The driver completes the delivery at position 2 (customer's delivery is now within next 3 stops)",
      "then": "The customer receives a notification that their delivery is approaching"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-6",
      "given": "A motorcycle-licensed driver in zone A and a pending freight delivery in zone A",
      "when": "The route optimizer attempts to build or recalculate the route",
      "then": "The freight delivery is excluded from the motorcycle driver's route and flagged as unassignable to this driver"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-7",
      "given": "Zone B has an operations override setting stop duration to 7 minutes",
      "when": "A route is calculated for a driver in zone B",
      "then": "Each stop in zone B uses 7 minutes (not the default 4) for ETA calculations"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-8",
      "given": "A driver starts their shift and there are zero pending deliveries in their zone",
      "when": "The shift-start event is processed",
      "then": "The driver is assigned an empty route and is placed in an idle/awaiting-deliveries state"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-9",
      "given": "A customer's delivery is at position 3 in the route (already within the next-3 window)",
      "when": "A recalculation moves it to position 6",
      "then": "No duplicate notification is sent; the customer's notification state reflects the updated position"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-10",
      "given": "Two drivers are active in the same zone and a new delivery arrives",
      "when": "The route optimizer processes the new delivery",
      "then": "The delivery is assigned to exactly one driver based on optimization criteria, and only that driver's route is recalculated"
    }
  ],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Driver starts shift with zero pending deliveries in zone", "expected_behavior": "Driver enters idle state with empty route. When a delivery appears in the zone, a route is generated and assigned." },
    { "scenario": "Multiple recalculation triggers fire simultaneously (new delivery added while another fails)", "expected_behavior": "Recalculations must be serialized or batched per driver to prevent race conditions. The final route must reflect all trigger events." },
    { "scenario": "Customer notification sent, then recalculation moves delivery outside next-3 window", "expected_behavior": "Notification already sent cannot be recalled. System should not re-notify when the delivery re-enters the window. Must track notification state per delivery, not per position." },
    { "scenario": "All deliveries in a zone require freight but the only active driver has a motorcycle license", "expected_behavior": "No deliveries are assigned. The system must surface an alert to operations that deliveries in the zone are unassignable due to vehicle class mismatch." },
    { "scenario": "Driver deviates but then returns to the original route", "expected_behavior": "System must define a deviation threshold (distance or time). A brief GPS drift should not trigger recalculation. Returning to route should cancel or supersede any in-progress recalculation." },
    { "scenario": "Route recalculation is triggered while a previous recalculation is still in progress", "expected_behavior": "The in-progress recalculation should be cancelled or the new trigger queued. The driver must never receive a stale route that doesn't account for the latest state." },
    { "scenario": "A delivery is added to the zone but the driver is currently on their last stop", "expected_behavior": "The new delivery becomes the driver's next (and only remaining) stop. No complex optimization needed but the recalculation trigger must still fire." },
    { "scenario": "Operations team changes the stop duration override mid-shift while drivers have active routes", "expected_behavior": "Spec is silent on this. Recommendation: recalculate ETAs for active routes using the new duration, but do not reorder stops to avoid driver confusion." },
    { "scenario": "Driver's shift ends while deliveries remain on route", "expected_behavior": "Spec does not address shift-end behavior. Remaining deliveries must return to the pending pool for reassignment. This is a critical gap." },
    { "scenario": "A delivery is reassigned from one zone to another while a driver has it on their active route", "expected_behavior": "Must trigger route recalculation for the original driver (delivery removed) and potentially for drivers in the new zone (delivery added)." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Route optimization directly reduces fuel costs, increases deliveries-per-shift (driver utilization), and reduces overtime. Vehicle class enforcement prevents failed delivery attempts that cost re-delivery fees. Accurate ETAs reduce customer compensation claims from missed windows.",
    "operational": "Automatic route assignment eliminates manual dispatch overhead. Dynamic recalculation keeps routes optimal despite real-world disruptions. Zone-level stop duration overrides give operations teams localized control (e.g., high-rise buildings take longer). Risk: frequent recalculations could create driver fatigue from constantly changing instructions.",
    "user": "Customers gain real-time proximity notifications (next-3-stops), improving availability for receiving deliveries and reducing failed attempts. Drivers get clear, optimized instructions reducing cognitive load. Risk: too-frequent route changes frustrate drivers who feel the system is indecisive.",
    "risk": "Vehicle class violation is a safety and regulatory risk with potential legal liability. Recalculation race conditions could produce invalid routes. Notification timing errors erode customer trust. Deviation detection false positives (GPS drift) could create unnecessary recalculations.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "Primary workflow change — shift from manual route planning to system-guided. Must trust system recommendations. Frequent recalculations may cause frustration." },
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "Receive proactive delivery proximity notifications. Expectation management improves but incorrect notifications erode trust." },
      { "group": "Operations Team", "impact": "Gains zone-level configuration control (stop duration override). Needs visibility into unassignable deliveries and vehicle class mismatches. Reduced manual dispatch work." },
      { "group": "Fleet Management", "impact": "Vehicle class data must be accurate and current. Incorrect vehicle class records create systemic assignment failures." },
      { "group": "Finance/Planning", "impact": "Fuel cost reduction and throughput increase are measurable. Need KPIs: deliveries-per-shift, fuel-per-delivery, on-time-delivery rate." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 55,
    "reasoning": "The spec covers the happy path well but has significant gaps: no definition of deviation detection threshold, no shift-end behavior, no multi-driver-per-zone assignment strategy, no recalculation concurrency model, no clarification on whether recalculation reorders already-notified stops, and no discussion of what happens when optimization is infeasible (all deliveries incompatible with driver). These gaps affect multiple bounded contexts.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Vehicle class constraint is a hard invariant — physical and legal",
      "Recalculation triggers are clearly enumerated (new delivery, failed delivery, deviation)",
      "Customer notification threshold (next 3 stops) is well-defined",
      "Stop duration default and override mechanism is clear"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Multi-driver zone assignment strategy — which driver gets a new delivery?",
      "Deviation detection definition — what constitutes a deviation vs GPS noise?",
      "Shift-end behavior — what happens to undelivered stops?",
      "Recalculation concurrency — how to handle multiple triggers in rapid succession",
      "Notification idempotency — what happens when recalculation reshuffles the next-3 window",
      "Mid-shift configuration changes — do active routes update immediately?"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "A driver is assigned to exactly one zone per shift",
      "Zone assignment is predetermined before shift start (not part of this feature)",
      "Vehicle class hierarchy exists and is well-defined (motorcycle < van < truck < freight)",
      "Route optimization means minimizing total travel time or distance, not other criteria",
      "Customer notification is sent once per delivery per approach — not re-sent on recalculation",
      "Deliveries have a required vehicle class attribute set at creation time"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "Multi-driver zone assignment: When multiple drivers are active in the same zone and a new delivery arrives, which driver gets it? The spec says 'all pending deliveries in their zone' at shift start but does not address competing drivers or load balancing.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Drivers", "Operations Team", "Customers"],
      "options": ["Assign to the driver whose route is most efficiently extended", "Assign to the driver with the fewest remaining stops", "Assign to the nearest driver by current position"],
      "recommendation": "Use route optimization score — assign to the driver whose total route cost increases the least. This preserves the optimization intent of the feature."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "Deviation detection threshold: What constitutes a 'manual deviation'? GPS noise, brief detours for fuel, or only explicit stop reordering? Without a threshold, false-positive recalculations will degrade driver experience.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Drivers", "Operations Team"],
      "options": ["Distance threshold (e.g., 500m off-route for 2+ minutes)", "Driver explicitly skips or reorders a stop in the app", "Both distance-based and explicit action"],
      "recommendation": "Combine both: explicit skip/reorder always triggers recalculation; GPS-based deviation requires a configurable distance+time threshold to filter noise."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "Shift-end behavior is completely unspecified. When a driver's shift ends with deliveries remaining on their route, the system must handle those deliveries — return to pool, assign to next shift, or alert operations. This is critical for delivery SLA compliance.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Drivers", "Customers", "Operations Team"],
      "options": ["Remaining deliveries auto-return to pending pool and are reassigned", "Driver must complete remaining stops (overtime)", "Operations team manually decides per delivery"],
      "recommendation": "Auto-return to pending pool with priority boost to prevent SLA violation. Alert operations if any delivery has been pending beyond SLA threshold."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "Notification idempotency on recalculation: If a customer was notified (delivery in next-3), then recalculation moves it outside the window, then it re-enters — does the customer get notified again? What if recalculation moves a delivery from position 4 to position 2 — should notification fire immediately or wait for the next stop completion?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers"],
      "options": ["Notify once per delivery, never re-notify regardless of position changes", "Notify each time delivery enters the window, with deduplication within a time window", "Notify once, but send an update if delivery is significantly delayed"],
      "recommendation": "Notify once per delivery. Track notification state on the delivery entity, not derived from route position. Prevents notification spam during frequent recalculations."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": []
}
